revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: 新規
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=香川縣護國神社| 画像=[[画像:Sanukinimiya.jpg|250px|]]
拝殿| 所在地=香川県善通寺市文京町4-5-5| 祭神=香川県出身の戦死者35700余柱| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社| 創建=明治31年（1898年）| 本殿=| 例祭=[[5月5日]]}} '''香川縣護國神社'''（かがわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]に鎮座する[[護国神社]]。'''讃岐宮'''（さぬきのみや）とも。境内面積は33.604m²。 == 沿革 == [[明治]]31年（[[1898年]]）、善通寺に[[日本陸軍|陸軍]]第11師団が設けられた際、招魂社を設置したのに始まる。[[昭和]]13年（[[1938年]]）、[[内務大臣]]の指定により護国神社となった。戦後荒廃が目立つようになったため改築に着手、昭和60年（[[1985年]]）に竣工した。 なお善通寺市内には現在[[陸上自衛隊]][[旅団#第14旅団 (自衛隊)|第14旅団]]善通寺駐屯地があり、旧陸軍時代に比べ規模は縮小したものの、「[[軍郷|軍都]]」的性格を引き続き有している。 == 境内 == *本殿 *拝殿 *社務所 *乃木神社 *史料館 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.sanukinomiya.com/ 公式サイト] [[Category:香川県の神社|かかわけんここくしんしや]] [[Category:護国神社|かかわけんここくしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: 画像追加
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=香川縣護國神社| 画像=[[画像:Sanukinimiya.jpg|250px|]]
拝殿| 所在地=香川県善通寺市文京町4-5-5| 祭神=香川県出身の戦死者35700余柱| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社| 創建=明治31年（1898年）| 本殿=| 例祭=[[5月5日]]}} '''香川縣護國神社'''（かがわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]に鎮座する[[護国神社]]。'''讃岐宮'''（さぬきのみや）とも。境内面積は33.604m²。 == 沿革 == [[明治]]31年（[[1898年]]）、善通寺に[[日本陸軍|陸軍]]第11師団が設けられた際、招魂社を設置したのに始まる。[[昭和]]13年（[[1938年]]）、[[内務大臣]]の指定により護国神社となった。戦後荒廃が目立つようになったため改築に着手、昭和60年（[[1985年]]）に竣工した。 なお善通寺市内には現在[[陸上自衛隊]][[旅団#第14旅団 (自衛隊)|第14旅団]]善通寺駐屯地があり、旧陸軍時代に比べ規模は縮小したものの、「[[軍郷|軍都]]」的性格を引き続き有している。 [[画像:神馬像.jpg|thumbnail|200px|神馬像]] == 境内 == *本殿 *拝殿 *社務所 *乃木神社 *史料館 ==交通アクセス== *[[土讃線]][[善通寺駅]]から西へ約900m。 *善通寺駅から善通寺市民バス「空海号」（東コース左廻り）に乗車、善通寺西高下車。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.sanukinomiya.com/ 公式サイト] [[Category:香川県の神社|かかわけんここくしんしや]] [[Category:護国神社|かかわけんここくしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: 位置
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=香川縣護國神社| 画像=[[画像:Sanukinimiya.jpg|250px|]]
拝殿| 所在地=香川県善通寺市文京町四丁目5番5号| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=護国の[[英霊]]| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社| 創建=明治31年（1898年）| 例祭=[[5月5日]]}} '''香川縣護國神社'''（かがわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。'''讃岐宮'''（さぬきのみや）とも称する。境内面積は33.604m²。 香川県出身の護国の[[英霊]]35700余柱を祀る。 == 沿革 == [[明治]]31年（[[1898年]]）、善通寺に[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]]第11師団が設けられた際、[[招魂社]]を設置したのに始まる。[[昭和]]13年（[[1938年]]）、[[内務大臣]]の指定により護国神社となった。戦後荒廃が目立つようになったため修復に着手、昭和60年（[[1985年]]）に竣工した。 なお善通寺市内には現在[[陸上自衛隊]][[旅団#第14旅団 (自衛隊)|第14旅団]][[善通寺駐屯地]]があり、旧陸軍時代に比べ規模は縮小したものの、「[[軍郷|軍都]]」的性格を引き続き有している。 == 境内 == [[画像:神馬像.jpg|thumbnail|240px|神馬像]] *本殿 *拝殿 *社務所 *[[乃木神社#善通寺市の乃木神社|乃木神社]] *史料館 *日本一社交通神社 *先賢堂 *警察消防招魂社 ==交通アクセス== *[[土讃線]][[善通寺駅]]から西へ約900m。 *善通寺駅から善通寺市民バス「空海号」（東コース左廻り）に乗車、善通寺西高下車。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.sanukinomiya.com/ 公式サイト] [[Category:香川県の神社|かかわけんここくしんしや]] [[Category:護国神社|かかわけんここくしんしや]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=香川県護国神社| 画像=[[画像:Sanukinimiya.jpg|250px|]]
拝殿| 所在地=香川県善通寺市文京町四丁目5番5号| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=護国の[[英霊]]| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社| 創建=明治31年（1898年）| 例祭=[[5月5日]]}} '''香川県護国神社'''（かがわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。'''讃岐宮'''（さぬきのみや）とも称する。境内面積は33.604m²。 香川県出身の護国の[[英霊]]35700余柱を祀る。 == 沿革 == [[明治]]31年（[[1898年]]）、善通寺に[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]]第11師団が設けられた際、[[招魂社]]を設置したのに始まる。[[昭和]]13年（[[1938年]]）、[[内務大臣]]の指定により護国神社となった。戦後荒廃が目立つようになったため修復に着手、昭和60年（[[1985年]]）に竣工した。 なお善通寺市内には現在[[陸上自衛隊]][[旅団#第14旅団 (自衛隊)|第14旅団]][[善通寺駐屯地]]があり、旧陸軍時代に比べ規模は縮小したものの、「[[軍郷|軍都]]」的性格を引き続き有している。 == 境内 == [[画像:神馬像.jpg|thumbnail|240px|神馬像]] *本殿 *拝殿 *社務所 *[[乃木神社#善通寺市の乃木神社|乃木神社]] *史料館 *日本一社交通神社 *先賢堂 *警察消防招魂社 ==交通アクセス== *[[土讃線]][[善通寺駅]]から西へ約900m。 *善通寺駅から善通寺市民バス「空海号」（東コース左廻り）に乗車、善通寺西高下車。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.sanukinomiya.com/ 公式サイト] [[Category:香川県の神社|かかわけんここくしんしや]] [[Category:護国神社|かかわけんここくしんしや]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=香川県護国神社| 画像=[[画像:Sanukinimiya.jpg|250px|]]
拝殿| 所在地=香川県善通寺市文京町四丁目5番5号| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=護国の[[英霊]]| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社| 創建=明治31年（1898年）| 例祭=[[5月5日]]}} '''香川県護国神社'''（かがわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。'''讃岐宮'''（さぬきのみや）とも称する。境内面積は33.604m²。 香川県出身の護国の[[英霊]]35700余柱を祀る。 == 沿革 == [[明治]]31年（[[1898年]]）、善通寺に[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]]第11師団が設けられた際、[[招魂社]]を設置したのに始まる。[[昭和]]13年（[[1938年]]）、[[内務大臣]]の指定により護国神社となった。戦後荒廃が目立つようになったため修復に着手、昭和60年（[[1985年]]）に竣工した。 なお善通寺市内には現在[[陸上自衛隊]][[旅団#第14旅団 (自衛隊)|第14旅団]][[善通寺駐屯地]]があり、旧陸軍時代に比べ規模は縮小したものの、「[[軍郷|軍都]]」的性格を引き続き有している。 == 境内 == [[画像:神馬像.jpg|thumbnail|240px|神馬像]] *本殿 *拝殿 *社務所 *[[乃木神社]] [[香川県]][[善通寺市]]にある。隣接する[[香川縣護國神社]]とともに「讃岐宮」と称される。[[昭和]]8年（[[1933年]]）創建。 善通寺市は明治31年に新設された[[第11師団 (日本軍)|陸軍第11師団]]のある地で、乃木はその初代師団長であった。 *史料館 *日本一社交通神社 *先賢堂 *警察消防招魂社 ==交通アクセス== *[[土讃線]][[善通寺駅]]から西へ約900m。 *善通寺駅から善通寺市民バス「空海号」（東コース左廻り）に乗車、善通寺西高下車。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.sanukinomiya.com/ 公式サイト] [[Category:香川県の神社|かかわけんここくしんしや]] [[Category:護国神社|かかわけんここくしんしや]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=香川県護国神社| 画像=[[画像:Sanukinimiya.jpg|250px|]]
拝殿| 所在地=香川県善通寺市文京町四丁目5番5号| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=護国の[[英霊]]| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社| 創建=明治31年（1898年）| 例祭=[[5月5日]]}} '''香川県護国神社'''（かがわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。'''讃岐宮'''（さぬきのみや）とも称する。境内面積は33.604m²。 香川県出身の護国の[[英霊]]35700余柱を祀る。 == 沿革 == [[明治]]31年（[[1898年]]）、善通寺に[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]]第11師団が設けられた際、[[招魂社]]を設置したのに始まる。[[昭和]]13年（[[1938年]]）、[[内務大臣]]の指定により護国神社となった。戦後荒廃が目立つようになったため修復に着手、昭和60年（[[1985年]]）に竣工した。 なお善通寺市内には現在[[陸上自衛隊]][[旅団#第14旅団 (自衛隊)|第14旅団]][[善通寺駐屯地]]があり、旧陸軍時代に比べ規模は縮小したものの、「[[軍郷|軍都]]」的性格を引き続き有している。 == 境内 == [[画像:神馬像.jpg|thumbnail|240px|神馬像]] *本殿 *拝殿 *社務所 *[[乃木神社]] -- [[乃木希典]]を祀る。[[昭和]]8年（[[1933年]]）創建。善通寺市は明治31年に新設された[[第11師団 (日本軍)|陸軍第11師団]]のある地で、乃木はその初代師団長であった。 *史料館 *日本一社交通神社 *先賢堂 *警察消防招魂社 ==交通アクセス== *[[土讃線]][[善通寺駅]]から西へ約900m。 *善通寺駅から善通寺市民バス「空海号」（東コース左廻り）に乗車、善通寺西高下車。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.sanukinomiya.com/ 公式サイト] [[Category:香川県の神社|かかわけんここくしんしや]] [[Category:護国神社|かかわけんここくしんしや]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=香川県護国神社| 画像=[[画像:Sanukinimiya.jpg|250px|]]
拝殿| 所在地=香川県善通寺市文京町四丁目5番5号| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=護国の[[英霊]]| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社| 創建=明治31年（1898年）| 例祭=[[5月5日]]}} '''香川県護国神社'''（かがわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。'''讃岐宮'''（さぬきのみや）とも称する。境内面積は33.604m²。 香川県出身の護国の[[英霊]]35700余柱を祀る。 == 沿革 == [[明治]]31年（[[1898年]]）、善通寺に[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]]第11師団が設けられた際、[[招魂社]]を設置したのに始まる。[[昭和]]13年（[[1938年]]）、[[内務大臣]]の指定により護国神社となった。戦後荒廃が目立つようになったため修復に着手、昭和60年（[[1985年]]）に竣工した。 なお善通寺市内には現在[[陸上自衛隊]][[旅団#第14旅団 (自衛隊)|第14旅団]][[善通寺駐屯地]]があり、旧陸軍時代に比べ規模は縮小したものの、「[[軍郷|軍都]]」的性格を引き続き有している。 == 境内 == [[画像:神馬像.jpg|thumbnail|240px|神馬像]] *本殿 *拝殿 *社務所 *[[乃木神社]] -- [[乃木希典]]を祀る。[[昭和]]8年（[[1933年]]）創建。善通寺市は明治31年に新設された[[第11師団 (日本軍)|陸軍第11師団]]のある地で、乃木はその初代師団長であった。 *史料館 *日本一社交通神社 *先賢堂 *警察消防招魂社 ==交通アクセス== *[[土讃線]][[善通寺駅]]から西へ約900m。 *善通寺駅から善通寺市民バス「空海号」（東コース左廻り）に乗車、善通寺西高下車。 ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-gokoku-jinja}} *[http://www.sanukinomiya.com/ 公式サイト] [[Category:香川県の神社|かかわけんここくしんしや]] [[Category:護国神社|かかわけんここくしんしや]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=香川県護国神社| 画像=[[画像:Sanukinimiya.jpg|250px|]]
拝殿| 所在地=香川県善通寺市文京町四丁目5番5号| 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 28| 経度度 = 133|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 48| 祭神=護国の[[英霊]]| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社| 別名=讃岐宮| 札所等=さぬき十五社12番| 創建=明治31年（1898年）| 例祭=[[5月5日]]}} '''香川県護国神社'''（かがわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。'''讃岐宮'''（さぬきのみや）とも称する。境内面積は33.604m²。 香川県出身の護国の[[英霊]]35700余柱を祀る。 == 沿革 == [[明治]]31年（[[1898年]]）、善通寺に[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]]第11師団が設けられた際、[[招魂社]]を設置したのに始まる。[[昭和]]13年（[[1938年]]）、[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]の指定により護国神社となった。戦後荒廃が目立つようになったため修復に着手、昭和60年（[[1985年]]）に竣工した。 なお善通寺市内には現在[[陸上自衛隊]][[旅団#第14旅団 (自衛隊)|第14旅団]][[善通寺駐屯地]]があり、旧陸軍時代に比べ規模は縮小したものの、「[[軍郷|軍都]]」的性格を引き続き有している。 == 境内 == [[画像:神馬像.jpg|thumbnail|神馬像]] [[画像:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|乃木神社]] *本殿 *拝殿 *社務所 *[[乃木神社]] -- [[乃木希典]]を祀る。[[昭和]]8年（[[1933年]]）創建。善通寺市は明治31年に新設された[[第11師団 (日本軍)|陸軍第11師団]]のある地で、乃木はその初代師団長であった。 *史料館 *日本一社交通神社 *先賢堂 *警察消防招魂社 ==交通アクセス== *[[土讃線]][[善通寺駅]]から西へ約900m。 *善通寺駅から善通寺市民バス「空海号」（東コース左廻り）に乗車、善通寺西高下車。 ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-gokoku-jinja}} *[http://www.sanukinomiya.com/ 公式サイト] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かかわけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:善通寺市の歴史]]

{{神社| 名称=香川県護国神社| 画像=[[画像:Sanukinimiya.jpg|250px|]]
拝殿| 所在地=香川県善通寺市文京町四丁目5番5号| 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 28| 経度度 = 133|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 48| 祭神=護国の[[英霊]]| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社| 別名=讃岐宮| 札所等=さぬき十五社12番| 創建=明治31年（1898年）| 例祭=[[5月5日]]}} '''香川県護国神社'''（かがわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。'''讃岐宮'''（さぬきのみや）とも称する。境内面積は33.604m²。 香川県出身の護国の[[英霊]]35700余柱を祀る。 == 沿革 == [[明治]]31年（[[1898年]]）、善通寺に[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]][[第11師団 (日本軍)|第11師団]]が設けられた際、[[招魂社]]を設置したのに始まる。[[昭和]]13年（[[1938年]]）、[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]の指定により護国神社となった。戦後荒廃が目立つようになったため修復に着手、昭和60年（[[1985年]]）に竣工した。 なお善通寺市内には現在[[陸上自衛隊]][[第14旅団 (陸上自衛隊)|第14旅団]][[善通寺駐屯地]]があり、旧陸軍時代に比べ規模は縮小したものの、「[[軍郷|軍都]]」的性格を引き続き有している。 == 境内 == [[画像:神馬像.jpg|thumbnail|神馬像]] [[画像:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|乃木神社]] *本殿 *拝殿 *社務所 *[[乃木神社]] -- [[乃木希典]]を祀る。[[昭和]]8年（[[1933年]]）創建。善通寺市は明治31年に新設された[[第11師団 (日本軍)|陸軍第11師団]]のある地で、乃木はその初代師団長であった。 *史料館 *日本一社交通神社 *先賢堂 *警察消防招魂社 ==交通アクセス== *[[土讃線]][[善通寺駅]]から西へ約900m。 *善通寺駅から善通寺市民バス「空海号」（東コース左廻り）に乗車、善通寺西高下車。 ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-gokoku-jinja}} *[http://www.sanukinomiya.com/ 公式サイト] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かかわけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:善通寺市の歴史]]

{{神社| 名称=香川県護国神社| 画像=[[画像:Sanukinimiya.jpg|250px|]]

{{神社| 名称=香川県護国神社| 画像=[[画像:Sanukinimiya.jpg|250px|]]
拝殿| 所在地=香川県善通寺市文京町四丁目5番5号| 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 28| 経度度 = 133|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 48| 祭神=護国の[[英霊]]| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社| 別名=讃岐宮| 札所等=さぬき十五社12番| 創建=明治31年（1898年）| 例祭=[[5月5日]]}} '''香川県護国神社'''（かがわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。'''讃岐宮'''（さぬきのみや）とも称する。境内面積は33.604m²。 香川県出身の護国の[[英霊]]35700余柱を祀る。 毎年8月には万灯夏まつり[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]（万灯みたま祭り）がおこなわれている。 == 沿革 == [[明治]]31年（[[1898年]]）、善通寺に[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]][[第11師団 (日本軍)|第11師団]]が設けられた際、[[招魂社]]を設置したのに始まる。[[昭和]]13年（[[1938年]]）、[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]の指定により護国神社となった。戦後荒廃が目立つようになったため修復に着手、昭和60年（[[1985年]]）に竣工した。 なお善通寺市内には現在[[陸上自衛隊]][[第14旅団 (陸上自衛隊)|第14旅団]][[善通寺駐屯地]]があり、旧陸軍時代に比べ規模は縮小したものの、「[[軍郷|軍都]]」的性格を引き続き有している。 == 境内 == [[画像:神馬像.jpg|thumbnail|神馬像]] [[画像:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|乃木神社]] *本殿 *拝殿 *社務所 *[[乃木神社]] -- [[乃木希典]]を祀る。[[昭和]]8年（[[1933年]]）創建。善通寺市は明治31年に新設された[[第11師団 (日本軍)|陸軍第11師団]]のある地で、乃木はその初代師団長であった。 *史料館 *日本一社交通神社 *先賢堂 *警察消防招魂社 ==交通アクセス== *[[土讃線]][[善通寺駅]]から西へ約900m。 *善通寺駅から善通寺市民バス「空海号」3号車」（西部・与北コース）に乗車、護国神社下車。 == 脚注 == ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} *[http://www.sanukinomiya.org/ 公式サイト] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かかわけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:善通寺市の歴史]]

{{神社| 名称=香川県護国神社| 画像=[[画像:Sanukinimiya.jpg|250px|]]
拝殿| 所在地=香川県善通寺市文京町四丁目5番5号| 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 28| 経度度 = 133|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 48| 祭神=護国の[[英霊]]| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社| 別名=讃岐宮| 札所等=さぬき十五社12番| 創建=明治31年（1898年）| 例祭=[[5月5日]]}} '''香川県護国神社'''（かがわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。'''讃岐宮'''（さぬきのみや）とも称する。境内面積は33.604m²。 香川県出身の護国の[[英霊]]35700余柱を祀る。 毎年8月には万灯夏まつり[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]（万灯みたま祭り）がおこなわれている。 == 沿革 == [[明治]]31年（[[1898年]]）、善通寺に[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]][[第11師団 (日本軍)|第11師団]]が設けられた際、[[招魂社]]を設置したのに始まる。[[昭和]]13年（[[1938年]]）、[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]の指定により護国神社となった。戦後荒廃が目立つようになったため修復に着手、昭和60年（[[1985年]]）に竣工した。 なお善通寺市内には現在[[陸上自衛隊]][[第14旅団 (陸上自衛隊)|第14旅団]][[善通寺駐屯地]]があり、旧陸軍時代に比べ規模は縮小したものの、「[[軍郷|軍都]]」的性格を引き続き有している。 2002年に乃木神社の本殿・拝殿・手水舎・鳥居・社務所が登録有形文化財に指定されたが[http://www.city.zentsuji.kagawa.jp/soshiki/50/bunkazai.html 善通寺ホームページ（文化財）]、2014年12月10日に社務所が全焼した[http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014年12月11日付）] 。 == 境内 == [[画像:神馬像.jpg|thumbnail|神馬像]] [[画像:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|乃木神社]] *本殿 *拝殿 *社務所 *[[乃木神社]] -- [[乃木希典]]を祀る。[[昭和]]8年（[[1933年]]）創建。善通寺市は明治31年に新設された[[第11師団 (日本軍)|陸軍第11師団]]のある地で、乃木はその初代師団長であった。 *史料館 *日本一社交通神社 *先賢堂 *警察消防招魂社 == 文化財 == ;登録有形文化財 *本殿 *拝殿 *手水舎 *鳥居 ;過去の登録有形文化財 *社務所（2014年12月10日に全焼した）[http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014年12月11日付）] ==交通アクセス== *[[土讃線]][[善通寺駅]]から西へ約900m。 *善通寺駅から善通寺市民バス「空海号」3号車」（西部・与北コース）に乗車、護国神社下車。 == 脚注 == ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} *[http://www.sanukinomiya.org/ 公式サイト] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かかわけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:善通寺市の歴史]]

revision.comment: 文化財名称。指定と登録の区別。

{{神社| 名称=香川県護国神社| 画像=[[画像:Sanukinimiya.jpg|250px|]]
拝殿| 所在地=香川県善通寺市文京町四丁目5番5号| 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 28| 経度度 = 133|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 48| 祭神=護国の[[英霊]]| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社| 別名=讃岐宮| 札所等=さぬき十五社12番| 創建=明治31年（1898年）| 例祭=[[5月5日]]}} '''香川県護国神社'''（かがわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。'''讃岐宮'''（さぬきのみや）とも称する。境内面積は33.604[[平方メートル|m2]]。 香川県出身の護国の[[英霊]]35700余柱を祀る。 毎年8月には万灯夏まつり[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]（万灯みたま祭り）がおこなわれている。 == 沿革 == [[明治]]31年（[[1898年]]）、善通寺に[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]][[第11師団 (日本軍)|第11師団]]が設けられた際、[[招魂社]]を設置したのに始まる。[[昭和]]13年（[[1938年]]）、[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]の指定により護国神社となった。戦後荒廃が目立つようになったため修復に着手、昭和60年（[[1985年]]）に竣工した。 なお善通寺市内には現在[[陸上自衛隊]][[第14旅団 (陸上自衛隊)|第14旅団]][[善通寺駐屯地]]があり、旧陸軍時代に比べ規模は縮小したものの、「[[軍郷|軍都]]」的性格を引き続き有している。 2002年に乃木神社の本殿・拝殿・手水舎・鳥居・社務所が[[登録有形文化財]]に登録されたが[http://www.city.zentsuji.kagawa.jp/soshiki/50/bunkazai.html 善通寺ホームページ（文化財）]、2014年12月10日に社務所が全焼した[http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014年12月11日付）] 。 == 境内 == [[画像:神馬像.jpg|thumbnail|神馬像]] [[画像:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|乃木神社（拝殿）]] *本殿 *拝殿 *社務所 *[[乃木神社]] -- [[乃木希典]]を祀る。[[昭和]]8年（[[1933年]]）創建。善通寺市は明治31年に新設された[[第11師団 (日本軍)|陸軍第11師団]]のある地で、乃木はその初代師団長であった。 *史料館 *日本一社交通神社 *先賢堂 *警察消防招魂社 == 文化財 == ;登録有形文化財 *乃木神社本殿 *乃木神社拝殿 *乃木神社手水舎 *乃木神社鳥居 ;過去の登録有形文化財 *乃木神社社務所（2014年12月10日に全焼した）[http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014年12月11日付）] ==交通アクセス== *[[土讃線]][[善通寺駅]]から西へ約900m。 *善通寺駅から善通寺市民バス「空海号」3号車」（西部・与北コース）に乗車、護国神社下車。 == 脚注 == ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} *[http://www.sanukinomiya.org/ 公式サイト] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かかわけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:善通寺市の建築物]] [[Category:善通寺市の歴史]] [[Category:香川県の登録有形文化財]]

{{神社| 名称=香川県護国神社| 画像=[[画像:Sanukinimiya.jpg|270px|]]
拝殿| 所在地=香川県善通寺市文京町四丁目5番5号| 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 28| 経度度 = 133|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 48| 地図=Japan Kagawa| 祭神=護国の[[英霊]]| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社| 別名=讃岐宮| 札所等=さぬき十五社12番| 創建=明治31年（1898年）| 例祭=[[5月5日]]}} '''香川県護国神社'''（かがわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。'''讃岐宮'''（さぬきのみや）とも称する。境内面積は33.604[[平方メートル|m2]]。 香川県出身の護国の[[英霊]]35700余柱を祀る。 毎年8月には万灯夏まつり[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]（万灯みたま祭り）がおこなわれている。 == 沿革 == [[明治]]31年（[[1898年]]）、善通寺に[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]][[第11師団 (日本軍)|第11師団]]が設けられた際、[[招魂社]]を設置したのに始まる。[[昭和]]13年（[[1938年]]）、[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]の指定により護国神社となった。戦後荒廃が目立つようになったため修復に着手、昭和60年（[[1985年]]）に竣工した。 なお善通寺市内には現在[[陸上自衛隊]][[第14旅団 (陸上自衛隊)|第14旅団]][[善通寺駐屯地]]があり、旧陸軍時代に比べ規模は縮小したものの、「[[軍郷|軍都]]」的性格を引き続き有している。 2002年に乃木神社の本殿・拝殿・手水舎・鳥居・社務所が[[登録有形文化財]]に登録されたが[http://www.city.zentsuji.kagawa.jp/soshiki/50/bunkazai.html 善通寺ホームページ（文化財）]、2014年12月10日に社務所が全焼した[http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014年12月11日付）] 。 == 境内 == [[画像:神馬像.jpg|thumbnail|神馬像]] [[画像:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|乃木神社（拝殿）]] *本殿 *拝殿 *社務所 *[[乃木神社]] -- [[乃木希典]]を祀る。[[昭和]]8年（[[1933年]]）創建。善通寺市は明治31年に新設された[[第11師団 (日本軍)|陸軍第11師団]]のある地で、乃木はその初代師団長であった。 *史料館 *日本一社交通神社 *先賢堂 *警察消防招魂社 == 文化財 == ;登録有形文化財 *乃木神社本殿 *乃木神社拝殿 *乃木神社手水舎 *乃木神社鳥居 ;過去の登録有形文化財 *乃木神社社務所（2014年12月10日に全焼した）[http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014年12月11日付）] ==交通アクセス== *[[土讃線]][[善通寺駅]]から西へ約900m。 *善通寺駅から善通寺市民バス「空海号」3号車」（西部・与北コース）に乗車、護国神社下車。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} *[http://www.sanukinomiya.org/ 公式サイト] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かかわけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:善通寺市の建築物]] [[Category:善通寺市の歴史]] [[Category:香川県の登録有形文化財]]

拝殿| 所在地=香川県善通寺市文京町四丁目5番5号| 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 28| 経度度 = 133|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 48| 地図=Japan Kagawa| 祭神=国事殉難者| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社| 別名=讃岐宮| 札所等=さぬき十五社12番| 創建=明治31年（1898年）| 例祭=[[5月5日]]}} '''香川県護国神社'''（かがわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。'''讃岐宮'''（さぬきのみや）とも称する。境内面積は33.604[[平方メートル|m2]]。 香川県出身の戦没者35700余柱を祀る。 毎年8月には万灯夏まつり[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]（万灯みたま祭り）がおこなわれている。 == 沿革 == [[明治]]31年（[[1898年]]）、善通寺に[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]][[第11師団 (日本軍)|第11師団]]が設けられた際、[[招魂社]]を設置したのに始まる。[[昭和]]13年（[[1938年]]）、[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]の指定により護国神社となった。戦後荒廃が目立つようになったため修復に着手、昭和60年（[[1985年]]）に竣工した。 なお善通寺市内には現在[[陸上自衛隊]][[第14旅団 (陸上自衛隊)|第14旅団]][[善通寺駐屯地]]があり、旧陸軍時代に比べ規模は縮小したものの、「[[軍郷|軍都]]」的性格を引き続き有している。 2002年に乃木神社の本殿・拝殿・手水舎・鳥居・社務所が[[登録有形文化財]]に登録されたが[http://www.city.zentsuji.kagawa.jp/soshiki/50/bunkazai.html 善通寺ホームページ（文化財）]、2014年12月10日に社務所が全焼した[http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014年12月11日付）] 。 == 境内 == [[画像:神馬像.jpg|thumbnail|神馬像]] [[画像:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|乃木神社（拝殿）]] *本殿 *拝殿 *社務所 *[[乃木神社]] -- [[乃木希典]]を祀る。[[昭和]]8年（[[1933年]]）創建。善通寺市は明治31年に新設された[[第11師団 (日本軍)|陸軍第11師団]]のある地で、乃木はその初代師団長であった。 *史料館 *日本一社交通神社 *先賢堂 *警察消防招魂社 == 文化財 == ;登録有形文化財 *乃木神社本殿 *乃木神社拝殿 *乃木神社手水舎 *乃木神社鳥居 ;過去の登録有形文化財 *乃木神社社務所（2014年12月10日に全焼した）[http://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014年12月11日付）] ==交通アクセス== *[[土讃線]][[善通寺駅]]から西へ約900m。 *善通寺駅から善通寺市民バス「空海号」3号車」（西部・与北コース）に乗車、護国神社下車。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} *[http://www.sanukinomiya.org/ 公式サイト] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かかわけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:善通寺市の建築物]] [[Category:善通寺市の歴史]] [[Category:香川県の登録有形文化財]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 08:59 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称=香川県護国神社 | 画像=[[画像:Sanukinimiya.jpg|270px|]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja shaden.jpg|270px|]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = 4-5-5 Bunkyōchō, Zentsūji, Kagawa Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Kagawa | deities = National heroes (''Kokushi Junnansha'') | shrine_rank = Gokoku Shrine designated by the Minister of the Interior | formal_name = Sanuki Shrine (讃岐宮) | pilgrimage = Sanuki Fifteen Shrines #12 | established = Meiji 31 (1898) | festivals = [[May 5]] }} '''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Zentsūji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture. Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama Festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern Festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]. == History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. Established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Main Hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014年12月11日付）] == Festivals == * Mantō Mitama Festival (Lantern Festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead. == Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[Zentsūji Station]] on the [[Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かがわけんごこくしんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Zentsūji]] [[Category:History of Zentsūji]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]]

Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Main Hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014年12月11日付）] == festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead. == Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[Zentsūji Station]] on the [[Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かがわけんごこくしんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:original pages]] {{Gokoku shrines}}[[category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Zentsūji]] [[Category:History of Zentsūji]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:original pages]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:original pages]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja shaden.jpg|270px|]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = 4-5-5 Bunkyōchō, Zentsūji, Kagawa Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Kagawa | deities = National heroes (''Kokushi Junnansha'') | shrine_rank = Gokoku Shrine designated by the Minister of the Interior | formal_name = Sanuki Shrine (讃岐宮) | pilgrimage = Sanuki Fifteen Shrines #12 | established = Meiji 31 (1898) | festivals = [[May 5]] }} '''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Zentsūji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture. Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]. == History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014年12月11日付）] == festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead. == Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[Zentsūji Station]] on the [[Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かがわけんごこくしんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:original pages]] {{Gokoku shrines}}[[category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Zentsūji]] [[Category:History of Zentsūji]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:original pages]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:original pages]]

It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture. Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]. == History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja shaden.jpg|270px|]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = 4-5-5 Bunkyōchō, Zentsūji, Kagawa Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Kagawa | deity = National heroes (''Kokushi Junnansha'') | shrine_rank = Gokoku Shrine designated by the Minister of the Interior | formal_name = Sanuki Shrine (讃岐宮) | pilgrimage = Sanuki Fifteen Shrines #12 | established = Meiji 31 (1898) | festivals = [[May 5]] }} '''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Zentsūji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture. Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]. == History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014年12月11日付）] == festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead. == Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[Zentsūji Station]] on the [[Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かがわけんごこくしんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}{{Gokoku shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Zentsūji]] [[Category:History of Zentsūji]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]]

Haiden (worship hall) | location = 4-5-5 Bunkyōchō, Zentsūji, Kagawa Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Kagawa | deity = National heroes (''Kokushi Junnansha'') | shrine_rank = Gokoku Shrine designated by the Minister of the Interior | formal_name = Sanuki Shrine (讃岐宮) | pilgrimage = Sanuki Fifteen Shrines #12 | established = Meiji 31 (1898) | festivals = [[May 5]] }} '''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Zentsūji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture. Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]. == History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; Haiden (worship hall) | location = 4-5-5 Bunkyōchō, Zentsūji, Kagawa Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Kagawa | deity = National heroes (''Kokushi Junnansha'') | shrine_rank = Gokoku Shrine designated by the Minister of the Interior | formal_name = Sanuki Shrine (讃岐宮) | pilgrimage = Sanuki Fifteen Shrines #12 | established = Meiji 31 (1898) | festivals = [[May 5]] }} '''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Zentsūji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture. Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]. == History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014年12月11日付）] == festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead. == Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[Zentsūji Station]] on the [[Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かがわけんごこくしんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}{{Gokoku shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Zentsūji]] [[Category:History of Zentsūji]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]]

Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Haiden (worship hall) | location = 4-5-5 Bunkyōchō, Zentsūji, Kagawa Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Kagawa | deity = National heroes (''Kokushi Junnansha'') | shrine_rank = Gokoku Shrine designated by the Minister of the Interior | formal_name = Sanuki Shrine (讃岐宮) | pilgrimage = Sanuki Fifteen Shrines #12 | established = Meiji 31 (1898) | festivals = [[May 5]] }} '''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Zentsūji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture. Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]. == History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture. Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]. == History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; Haiden (worship hall) | location = 4-5-5 Bunkyōchō, Zentsūji, Kagawa Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Kagawa | deity = National heroes (''Kokushi Junnansha'') | shrine_rank = Gokoku Shrine designated by the Minister of the Interior | formal_name = Sanuki Shrine (讃岐宮) | pilgrimage = Sanuki Fifteen Shrines #12 | established = Meiji 31 (1898) | festivals = [[May 5]] }} '''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Zentsūji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture. Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]. == History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014年12月11日付）] == festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead. == Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[Zentsūji Station]] on the [[Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かがわけんごこくしんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}{{Gokoku shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Zentsūji]] [[Category:History of Zentsūji]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] \[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]]

Haiden (worship hall) | location = 4-5-5 Bunkyōchō, Zentsūji, Kagawa Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Kagawa | deity = National heroes (''Kokushi Junnansha'') | shrine_rank = Gokoku Shrine designated by the Minister of the Interior | formal_name = Sanuki Shrine (讃岐宮) | pilgrimage = Sanuki Fifteen Shrines #12 | established = Meiji 31 (1898) | festivals = [[May 5]] }} '''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Zentsūji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture. Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]. == History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014年12月11日付）] == festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead. == Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[Zentsūji Station]] on the [[Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かがわけんごこくしんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}{{Gokoku shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Zentsūji]] [[Category:History of Zentsūji]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] \[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:New]]

Haiden (worship hall) | location = 4-5-5 Bunkyōchō, Zentsūji, Kagawa Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Kagawa | deity = National heroes (''Kokushi Junnansha'') | shrine_rank = Gokoku Shrine designated by the Minister of the Interior | formal_name = Sanuki Shrine (讃岐宮) | pilgrimage = Sanuki Fifteen Shrines #12 | established = Meiji 31 (1898) | festivals = [[May 5]] }} '''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Zentsūji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture. Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]. == History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014th year 12月11日付）] == festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead. == Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[Zentsūji Station]] on the [[Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かがわけんごこくしんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}{{Gokoku shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Zentsūji]] [[Category:History of Zentsūji]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] \[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]][[Category:New]] [[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja shaden.jpg|270px|]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = 4-5-5 Bunkyōchō, Zentsūji, Kagawa Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Kagawa | deity = National heroes (''Kokushi Junnansha'') | shrine_rank = Gokoku Shrine designated by the Minister of the Interior | formal_name = Sanuki Shrine (讃岐宮) | pilgrimage = Sanuki Fifteen Shrines #12 | established = Meiji 31 (1898) | festivals = [[May 5]] }} '''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Zentsūji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture. Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]. == History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014th year 12月11日付）] == festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead. == Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[Zentsūji Station]] on the [[Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{Gokoku shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|かがわけんごこくしんじゃ}} {{citation needed|Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Zentsūji]] [[Category:History of Zentsūji]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]]

Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014th year 12月11日付）] == festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead. == Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[Zentsūji Station]] on the [[Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{Gokoku shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{citation needed|かがわけんごこくしんじゃ}} {{citation needed|Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Zentsūji]] [[Category:History of Zentsūji]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]]

Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja shaden.jpg|270px|]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = 4-5-5 Bunkyōchō, Zentsūji, Kagawa Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Kagawa | deity = National heroes (''Kokushi Junnansha'') | shrine_Rank = Gokoku Shrine designated by the Minister of the Interior | formal_name = Sanuki Shrine (讃岐宮) | pilgrimage = Sanuki Fifteen Shrines #12 | established = Meiji 31 (1898) | festivals = [[May 5]] }} '''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Zentsūji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture. Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]. == History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014th year 12月11日付）] == festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead. == Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[Zentsūji Station]] on the [[Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{Gokoku shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{citation needed|かがわけんごこくしんじゃ}} {{citation needed|Kagawa Gokoku Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Zentsūji]] [[Category:History of Zentsūji]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja shaden.jpg|270px|]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = 4-5-5 Bunkyōchō, Zentsūji, Kagawa Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Kagawa | deity = National heroes (''Kokushi Junnansha'') | shrine_Rank = Gokoku Shrine designated by the Minister of the Interior | formal_name = Sanuki Shrine (讃岐宮) | pilgrimage = Sanuki Fifteen Shrines #12 | established = Meiji 31 (1898) | festivals = [[May 5]] }} '''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Zentsūji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture. Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]. == History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[:en:11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014th year 12月11日付）] == festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead. == Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[Zentsūji Station]] on the [[Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{Gokoku shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Zentsūji]] [[Category:History of Zentsūji]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]]

Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[:en:14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014th year 12月11日付）] == festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead. == Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[Zentsūji Station]] on the [[:en:Dosan Line|Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{Gokoku shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]]

Haiden (worship hall) | location = 4-5-5 Bunkyōchō, Zentsūji, Kagawa Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Kagawa | deity = National heroes (''Kokushi Junnansha'') | shrine_Rank = Gokoku Shrine designated by the Minister of the Interior | formal_name = Sanuki Shrine (讃岐宮) | pilgrimage = Sanuki Fifteen Shrines #12 | established = Meiji 31 (1898) | festivals = [[May 5]] }} '''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Zentsūji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture. Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]. == History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[:en:11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[:en:14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014th year 12月11日付）] == festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead. == Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[Zentsūji Station]] on the [[:en:Dosan Line|Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{Gokoku shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja shaden.jpg|270px|]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = 4-5-5 Bunkyōchō, Zentsūji, Kagawa Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|13|28|N|133|46|48|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Kagawa | deity = National heroes (''Kokushi Junnansha'') | shrine_Rank = Gokoku Shrine designated by the Minister of the Interior | formal_name = Sanuki Shrine (讃岐宮) | pilgrimage = Sanuki Fifteen Shrines #12 | established = Meiji 31 (1898) | festivals = [[May 5]] }} '''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Zentsūji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture. Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]. == History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[:en:11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[World War II]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[:en:14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014th year 12月11日付）] == festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead. == Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[Zentsūji Station]] on the [[:en:Dosan Line|Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{Gokoku shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[Category:Qq]][[ja:香川縣護國神社]] [[Category:善通寺市の建築物]] [[Category:善通寺市の歴史]] [[Category:香川県の登録有形文化財]]

revision.comment: 41 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:香川縣護國神社 up to rev 99362334
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 08:59 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称=香川県護国神社 | 画像=[[画像:Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja shaden.jpg|270px|]]
拝殿 | 所在地=香川県善通寺市文京町四丁目5番5号 | 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 13|緯度秒 = 28 | 経度度 = 133|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 48 | 地図=Japan Kagawa | 祭神=国事殉難者 | 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社 | 正式名称=讃岐宮 | 札所等=さぬき十五社12番 | 創建=明治31年（1898年） | 例祭=[[5月5日]] }} '''香川県護国神社'''（かがわけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。正式名称は'''讃岐宮'''（さぬきぐう）。境内面積は33.604[[平方メートル|m2]]。 香川県出身の戦没者35700余柱を祀る。 毎年8月には万灯夏まつり[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]（万灯みたま祭り）がおこなわれている。 == 沿革 == [[明治]]31年（[[1898年]]）、善通寺に[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]][[第11師団 (日本軍)|第11師団]]が設けられた際、[[招魂社]]を設置したのに始まる。[[昭和]]13年（[[1938年]]）、[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]の指定により護国神社となった。戦後荒廃が目立つようになったため修復に着手、昭和60年（[[1985年]]）に竣工した。 なお善通寺市内には現在[[陸上自衛隊]][[第14旅団 (陸上自衛隊)|第14旅団]][[善通寺駐屯地]]があり、旧陸軍時代に比べ規模は縮小したものの、「[[軍郷|軍都]]」的性格を引き続き有している。 2002年に乃木神社の本殿・拝殿・手水舎・鳥居・社務所が[[登録有形文化財]]に登録されたが[http://www.city.zentsuji.kagawa.jp/soshiki/50/bunkazai.html 善通寺ホームページ（文化財）]、2014年12月10日に社務所が全焼した[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014年12月11日付）] 。 == 境内 == [[画像:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|神馬像]] [[画像:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|乃木神社（拝殿）]] *本殿 *拝殿 *社務所 *[[乃木神社]] -- [[乃木希典]]を祀る。[[昭和]]8年（[[1933年]]）創建。善通寺市は明治31年に新設された[[第11師団 (日本軍)|陸軍第11師団]]のある地で、乃木はその初代師団長であった。 *史料館 *日本一社交通神社 *先賢堂 *警察消防招魂社 == 文化財 == ;登録有形文化財 *乃木神社本殿 *乃木神社拝殿 *乃木神社手水舎 *乃木神社鳥居 ;過去の登録有形文化財 *乃木神社社務所（2014年12月10日に全焼した）[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014年12月11日付）] ==交通アクセス== *[[土讃線]][[善通寺駅]]から西へ約900m。 *善通寺駅から善通寺市民バス「空海号」3号車」（西部・与北コース）に乗車、護国神社下車。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かかわけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:善通寺市の建築物]] [[Category:善通寺市の歴史]] [[Category:香川県の登録有形文化財]]
