It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture. Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]. == History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[:en:11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938. Post-[[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[:en:Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[:en:14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base. == Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[:en:Nogi Shrine|Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine === Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014th year 12月11日付）] == festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead. == Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[:en:Zentsūji Station|Zentsūji Station]] on the [[:en:Dosan Line|Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{Gokoku shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Kagawa Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:香川縣護國神社]] [[Category:善通寺市の建築物]] [[Category:善通寺の Municipal History]] [[Category:香川県の登録有形文化財]] {{Commons category|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} [[Category:Pages with added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|香川縣護國神社|version=99362334|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Zentsūji|Zentsūji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture.
Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen<ref>[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]</ref>.
== History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[:en:11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938.
Post-[[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[:en:Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[:en:14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base.
== Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[:en:Nogi Shrine|Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine
=== Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)<ref name="fire">[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014th year 12月11日付）]</ref>
== festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead.
== Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[:en:Zentsūji Station|Zentsūji Station]] on the [[:en:Dosan Line|Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{Gokoku shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}}

'''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Zentsūji|Zentsūji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture.
Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen<ref>[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]</ref>.

Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938.
Post-[[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[:en:Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[:en:14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base.

Post-[[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[:en:Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[:en:14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base.

== History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[:en:11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[:en:Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[:en:14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base.

It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture.
Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen<ref>[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]</ref>.
== History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[:en:11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938.
Post-[[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[:en:Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[:en:14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base.
== Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[:en:Nogi Shrine|Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine
=== Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)<ref name="fire">[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014th year 12月11日付）]</ref>
== festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead.
== Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[:en:Zentsūji Station|Zentsūji Station]] on the [[:en:Dosan Line|Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{Gokoku shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}}

'''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Zentsūji|Zentsūji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture.
Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen<ref>[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]</ref>.
== History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[:en:11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938.
Post-[[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[:en:Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[:en:14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base.
== Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[:en:Nogi Shrine|Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine
=== Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)<ref name="fire">[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014th year 12月11日付）]</ref>
== festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead.
== Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[:en:Zentsūji Station|Zentsūji Station]] on the [[:en:Dosan Line|Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:香川縣護國神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:香川縣護國神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:香川縣護國神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:香川縣護國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11667575]] Wikidata name: Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11667575]] Wikidata name: Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = File:Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja shaden.jpg | location = 4-5-5 Bunkyōchō, Zentsūji, Kagawa Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.224444444444444|N|133.78|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Kagawa | deity = National heroes (''Kokushi Junnansha'') | shrine_Rank = Gokoku Shrine designated by the Minister of the Interior | formal_name = Sanuki Shrine (讃岐宮) | pilgrimage = Sanuki Fifteen Shrines #12 | established = Meiji 31 (1898) | festivals = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] | prefecture= {{ill|Zentsuji|WD=Q191300}} }}
'''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Zentsūji|Zentsūji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture.
Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen<ref>[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]</ref>.
== History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]’s [[:en:11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|11th Division]] in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938.
Post-[[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the [[:en:Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|JSDF]]’s [[:en:14th Brigade (Japan)|14th Brigade]] at the Zentsūji Base.
== Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[:en:Nogi Shrine|Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine
=== Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)<ref name="fire">[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014th year 12月11日付）]</ref>
== festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead.
== Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of [[:en:Zentsūji Station|Zentsūji Station]] on the [[:en:Dosan Line|Dosan Line]]. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{Gokoku shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}}
\

'''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Zentsūji|en|Zentsūji|WD=Q8069254|lt=Zentsūji}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture.
Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen<ref>[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]</ref>.
== History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|WD=Q276535|lt=Imperial Japanese Army}}’s {{ill|11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|en|11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|WD=Q700269|lt=11th Division}} in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938.
Post-{{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}, the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|WD=Q889751|lt=JSDF}}’s {{ill|14th Brigade (Japan)|en|14th Brigade (Japan)|WD=Q4550000|lt=14th Brigade}} at the Zentsūji Base.
== Grounds == [[File:Shinme - KagawaKen-Gokoku-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Sacred horse statue]] [[File:Nogi-jinja Zentsuji.jpeg|thumbnail|Nogi Shrine (haiden)]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall (''haiden'') * Shrine office (''shamusho'') * [[:en:Nogi Shrine|Nogi Shrine]] – Dedicated to [[Nogi Maresuke]], the first commander of the 11th Division. established in 1933. * Historical museum * Japan Traffic Shrine (''Nihon Issha Kōtsū Jinja'') * Hall of Sages (''Senkendō'') * Police and Firefighters Memorial Shrine
=== Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)<ref name="fire">[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014th year 12月11日付）]</ref>
== festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead.
== Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of {{ill|Zentsūji Station|en|Zentsūji Station|WD=Q899921|lt=Zentsūji Station}} on the {{ill|Dosan Line|en|Dosan Line|WD=Q861077|lt=Dosan Line}}. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja}} * {{Official website|name=香川縣護國神社}} {{Gokoku shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}}

Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938.
Post-{{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|WD=Q889751|lt=JSDF|1=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force}}’s {{ill|14th Brigade (Japan)|en|14th Brigade (Japan)|WD=Q4550000|lt=14th Brigade|1=14th Brigade (Japan)}} at the Zentsūji Base.

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = File:Kagawaken-Gokoku-jinja shaden.jpg | location = 4-5-5 Bunkyōchō, Zentsūji, Kagawa Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.224444444444444|N|133.78|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Kagawa | deity = National heroes (''Kokushi Junnansha'') | shrine_Rank = Gokoku Shrine designated by the Minister of the Interior | formal_name = Sanuki Shrine (讃岐宮) | pilgrimage = Sanuki Fifteen Shrines #12 | established = Meiji 31 (1898) | festivals = {{ill|May 5|en|May 5|qid=Q2550|lt=May 5|1=May 5}} | prefecture= {{ill|Zentsuji|qid=Q191300|1=Zentsūji, Kagawa}} }}
'''Kagawa Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (香川県護国神社, ''Kagawaken Gokoku Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Zentsūji|en|Zentsūji|qid=Q8069254|lt=Zentsūji|1=Zentsūji}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is officially named '''Sanuki Shrine''' (讃岐宮) and occupies an area of 33,604 square meters. The shrine honors over 35,700 war dead from Kagawa Prefecture.
Each August, the shrine hosts the ''Mantō Mitama festival'' (万灯みたま祭り), also known as the Lantern festival, in memory of the spirits of the fallen<ref>[https://37magazine.jp/article_detail.aspx?id=73 かがわのみんなのリアルマガジン]</ref>.
== History == The shrine was established in 1898, coinciding with the establishment of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|qid=Q276535|lt=Imperial Japanese Army|1=Imperial Japanese Army}}’s {{ill|11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|en|11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)|qid=Q700269|lt=11th Division|1=11th Division (Imperial Japanese Army)}} in Zentsūji. Originally a ''Shōkonsha'' (招魂社), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of the Interior in 1938.
Post-{{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, the shrine fell into disrepair but underwent extensive restoration, completed in 1985. Zentsūji, although reduced in scale compared to its military prominence during the Imperial era, retains aspects of its ''gun-to'' (軍都, "military town") heritage, currently hosting the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|qid=Q889751|lt=JSDF|1=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force}}’s {{ill|14th Brigade (Japan)|en|14th Brigade (Japan)|qid=Q4550000|lt=14th Brigade|1=14th Brigade (Japan)}} at the Zentsūji Base.

=== Cultural Properties === ; Registered Tangible Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine main hall * Nogi Shrine Worship Hall * Nogi Shrine Ablution Pavilion (''temizuya'') * Nogi Shrine Torii Gate ; Former Registered Cultural Properties * Nogi Shrine Office (destroyed by fire on December 10, 2014)<ref name="fire">[https://www.shikoku-np.co.jp/kagawa_news/social/20141211000163 四国新聞社（2014th year 12月11日付）]</ref>
== festivals == * Mantō Mitama festival (Lantern festival) – Held annually in August to honor the spirits of the war dead.
== Access == * Approx. 900 meters west of {{ill|Zentsūji Station|en|Zentsūji Station|qid=Q899921|lt=Zentsūji Station|1=Zentsūji Station}} on the {{ill|Dosan Line|en|Dosan Line|qid=Q861077|lt=Dosan Line|1=Dosan Line}}. * City bus (Zentsūji Community Bus "Kūkai-go" Line 3, West and Yonaka Route): Disembark at Gokoku Shrine.
